title: Red Hat Security Advisory: postgresql:13 security update
overview:
An update for the postgresql:13 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.4 Advanced Mission Critical Update Support and Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.4 Extended Update Support Long-Life Add-On.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: postgresql:13 security update

An update for the postgresql:13 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.4 Advanced Mission Critical Update Support and Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.4 Extended Update Support Long-Life Add-On.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for the postgresql:13 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.4 Advanced Mission Critical Update Support and Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.4 Extended Update Support Long-Life Add-On.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
b821c30a.src.rpm
6d959a24.src.rpm
b821c30a.x86_64.rpm
6d959a24.x86_64.rpm
6d959a24.noarch.rpm

CVE-2025-12817: A vulnerability has been identified in PostgreSQL’s CREATE STATISTICS command where the database does not check that the user has the required schema CREATE privilege. A table owner user could create a statistics object in any schema, blocking other users who legitimately hold CREATE STATISTICS permissions from creating objects with the same name. This results in a denial-of-service of the statistics creation functionality.

CVE-2025-12818: A vulnerability has been identified in PostgreSQL’s libpq client library, where integer wraparound in several allocation-size calculations allows a peer or input provider to cause an undersized buffer and then write out-of-bounds by hundreds of megabytes. This can lead to a client application segmentation fault or crash when using libpq to connect to a PostgreSQL server.
